items.note: The financial vehicle (new or existing) through which GCF finance is channelled to subprojects at local/regional/national level under an EDA project. [no ZH parallel — pending GCF translation team]

[no ZH parallel — pending GCF translation team] [publications variant: the SAP Programming Guidelines acronym list expands iTAP/ITAP as 'Independent Technical Assessment Panel' (vs the glossed 'Advisory Panel') — same body, publication-side wording variant; not a separate entry per Simon (2026-06-20).]
Application process for micro-scale projects/programmes requiring a GCF contribution up to USD 10 million with very minimal E&S risks. [no ZH parallel — pending GCF translation team]
A locally selected project financed through the EDA facility; screening/assessment/selection devolved to regional/national/subnational level. [no ZH parallel — pending GCF translation team]
Established financial mechanism (trust funds, national funding facilities, budget allocations) set up to fund subprojects at national/local level. [no ZH parallel — pending GCF translation team]

A request for submission of funding proposals from direct access entities, with specific eligibility standards, project requirements and an allocated budget envelope. [no ZH parallel — pending GCF translation team]

Document annexed to the EDA funding proposal explaining how GCF requirements are met at subproject level; a facility management tool used during implementation. [no ZH parallel — pending GCF translation team]

Fund-manager unit (hosted by AE or EE) managing daily operations of the EDA financial vehicle; acts as secretariat of the oversight and decision-making bodies. [no ZH parallel — pending GCF translation team]

EDA decision-making body — representatives of involved institutions incl. NDA, beneficiaries, civil society, private sector, academia — assessing/selecting subprojects. [no ZH parallel — pending GCF translation team]

EDA governance function providing strategic guidance to the AE, reviewing AE reporting, field visits and stakeholder communication; must avoid conflict of interest with decision-makers. [no ZH parallel — pending GCF translation team]
EDA governance body (often the steering committee) responsible for screening, assessment and selection of subprojects under the CfP modality. [no ZH parallel — pending GCF translation team]
Committee/group representing the direct and indirect beneficiaries in communities impacted by the project/programme. [no ZH parallel — pending GCF translation team]
EDA-level function to handle disputes/complaints from beneficiaries/stakeholders re: subprojects, typically reporting to the EDA steering committee. [no ZH parallel — pending GCF translation team]

Fiduciary standards AEs may be accredited for: (i) grant award/funding allocation mechanisms; (ii) on-lending/blending; (iii) project management. Only DAEs with (i)/(ii) may submit EDA projects. [no ZH parallel — pending GCF translation team]

items.en_term: sexual exploitation, sexual abuse and sexual harassment
items.note: Defined GCF acronym; GCF has zero tolerance for SEAH in Fund-related activities. (Defined identically in the Revised E&S Policy and the SEAH Policy.)

Defined scope of the SEAH Policy: Co-Chairs of the Board, Board Members, Alternate Members, Advisers, Board-appointed Officials, External Members, and GCF Personnel. NB a broader 'Covered Individual' set is defined in the integrity policies (Prohibited Practices) — same term, policy-specific scope.

items.note: The Executive Director and the Heads of the Independent Evaluation Unit (IEU), Independent Integrity Unit (IIU) and Independent Redress Mechanism (IRM), who are appointed by the Board.

Any activity financed, administered or supported by the Fund (with its own or others' resources), or any activity that materially affects or may be relevant to the Fund. Broader than 'GCF-financed activities'. (Same defined term recurs across the integrity policies — Prohibited Practices, Whistleblower, AML/CFT.)

items.note: Sworn written disclosure of a person's history of SEAH (lawfully disclosable convictions or disciplinary measures), required of Board-appointed Officials, External Members and GCF Personnel before appointment. [review: FR OCR doubling artefact corrected by agent to 'Déclaration SEAH'.]

zh.t: 道德操守和审计委员会

items.note: GCF Board committee responsible for ethics/integrity oversight; with the IIU handles SEAH reports regarding senior officials, approves AML/CFT exceptions and SOPs, addresses certain Wrongdoing cases, and presents implementation reports to the Board. (Established by decision B.05/13(e).)
An expert serving as an external member on a panel or group established by the GCF Board; within the scope of the SEAH Policy as a Covered Individual.
Any GCF Staff and any other individual contracted/engaged by GCF to perform official functions, excluding Board-appointed Officials and External Members.

As described in the GCF ESS standards: the prioritized steps for limiting adverse environmental and social impacts through avoidance, minimization, restoration and compensation, plus opportunities for sustainable development benefits.

Facilities not funded as part of a GCF project but that would not have been constructed/expanded without it, and without which the project would not be viable; relevant to E&S risk/impact scope and must meet GCF ESS standards.

items.en_term: investment framework for GCF-1 (initial investment framework)
items.note: GCF's initial investment framework comprising investment policies, investment strategy and portfolio targets, and investment guidelines (6 criteria, 24 coverage areas); updated by decision B.27/06 for the GCF first replenishment period. NB distinct from existing 'Investment framework' / 'Investment Framework for GCF-2' entries — this is the GCF-1-period version; see also 'initial investment framework' (Governing/B.07/06 founding form).

GCF-1 allocation parameters with associated targets (e.g. 50/50 mitigation/adaptation balance over time; 50% adaptation floor for vulnerable countries; PSF allocation >20%) used to steer the GCF portfolio during a replenishment period.

Parameters, determined in grant equivalents, that govern how GCF resources are allocated across mitigation/adaptation, vulnerable countries, direct access, private sector etc. during a replenishment period.

Accounting basis in which GCF funding (grants, concessional loans, other instruments) is measured: the grant-equivalent subsidy element; GCF allocation parameters and targets are determined in grant equivalents.

Formal Co-financing Policy definition: the financial resources (Public Finance or Private Finance) required in addition to the GCF Proceeds to implement the Funded Activity. DISTINCT, formal definition kept alongside the existing generic 'Co-financing' key term (Programming Manual) — see note on that entry.
Amount of Co-financing, based on ex-ante estimations, identified in the Funding Proposal and included in the FAA, expected to be necessary to implement the Funded Activity.
Amount of Co-financing, based on ex-post assessment, actually provided to the Funded Activity during its implementation.
In the context of a Funded Activity, the Private Finance mobilized as a result of the GCF Proceeds, calculated per relevant GCF methodologies.

ru.t: Частное финансирование с использованием заемных средств

items.note: Private investment resulting from GCF involvement in an investment, whether or not GCF was directly involved in raising it, including investment by additional investors after the first project completes.
Financial resources that flow alongside GCF Proceeds to a project but are not required to implement the Funded Activity and are earmarked for other outcomes.
All financial resources for implementing a Funded Activity from entities >50% owned/controlled by private shareholders (GCF Co-financing Policy definition).

All financial resources (other than GCF Proceeds) for implementing a Funded Activity from the public sector or entities >50% owned/controlled by the public sector (GCF Co-financing Policy definition).

Set of indicators (adopted B.22/15) for each of the six GCF investment criteria; used by AEs/TAP/Secretariat/Board to describe how a funding proposal delivers against the criteria; not a binary pass/fail screen.

fr.t: Financement PPF (modalité de financement du PPF)

items.note: PPF support modality where AEs receive funds from GCF to undertake project-preparation activities themselves, organising procurement/implementation and accountable for oversight and reporting. [no RU/ZH parallel — PPF Guidelines published EN/FR/ES/AR only — pending GCF translation team]

PPF support modality where GCF deploys an independent consultancy firm from its project-preparation roster directly to provide preparation services to an AE. [no RU/ZH parallel — PPF Guidelines published EN/FR/ES/AR only — pending GCF translation team]

Streamlined PPF window for AE requests up to USD 300,000 (incl. AE fee and PMC), using a shortened template for quicker preparation and approval. [no RU/ZH parallel — PPF Guidelines published EN/FR/ES/AR only — pending GCF translation team]

Fee (up to 8.5% under the PPF) paid to the accredited entity, calculated on the total budget excluding co-financing, to support implementation of GCF projects and eligible GCF-related activities. [no RU/ZH parallel — PPF Guidelines published EN/FR/ES/AR only — pending GCF translation team]

Direct administrative costs to execute a GCF project (typically a dedicated PMU), shown as a separate budget component; GCF share capped (under the PPF up to 7.5% of total GCF-financed budget plus contingency; 5%/7.5% thresholds trigger detailed documentation). [no RU/ZH parallel — PPF Guidelines published EN/FR/ES/AR only — pending GCF translation team]

Umbrella agreement setting terms/conditions between GCF and delivery partners for implementing a portfolio of readiness support requests and PPF applications. [no RU/ZH parallel — PPF Guidelines published EN/FR/ES/AR only — pending GCF translation team]

Risk-based process by which an AE seeks to maintain its accredited status at the end of its five-year accreditation term (application due >=6 months before term end); may be same scope, upgrade or downgrade. NB existing key-term 'Re-accreditation process' (B.42) describes the same process from the Accreditation Framework angle.
Fixed five-year period of an entity's accreditation, beginning when the signed Accreditation Master Agreement becomes effective.

es.t: revisión de acreditación de mitad de período

items.note: Review conducted by GCF at approximately 2.5 years into an AE's accreditation term assessing changes in institutional systems, policies, procedures and capacities.
Self-assessment submitted annually by an AE (using the GCF template) reporting whether there have been changes affecting its ability to meet its AE role/AMA obligations.
The dimensions defining what an AE is accredited for: maximum financing size category, environmental and social safeguards risk category, and geographic scope (national/regional).

Any modification to a funded activity after execution of the FAA (GCF Policy on Restructuring and Cancellation definition). NB existing acronym PRC = Policy on Restructuring and Cancellation is the parent policy.

A proposed change to an approved funding proposal/funded activity meeting defined thresholds (e.g. inconsistency with GCF mandate, material adverse deviation in climate outcomes, change in E&S category from lower to higher) requiring Board approval.
A Board decision approving a funding proposal, including relevant annexes and referenced documents such as the related term sheet.

A relinquishment of, material deviation from, or other deviation from a condition set forth in the Approval Decision, funding proposal or term sheet; requires Board approval unless delegated to the ED.

Cancellation of a project/programme as a consequence of the Board's decision not to approve a Major Change (where the AE does not proceed on the existing Approval Decision or withdraw the FP within 120 days).

items.note: Mandatory initial assessment of the gender baseline situation that the AE undertakes during project preparation; basis for the project-level gender action plan.

items.en_term: impact potential, paradigm shift, sustainable development, country ownership, efficiency and effectiveness, needs of the recipient
items.note: Collective shorthand for compliance with GCF's six investment criteria: Impact Potential, Paradigm Shift, Sustainable development potential, Country ownership, Efficiency and effectiveness, and Needs of the recipient. [RU not-found — IPSCEN abbreviation not used in RU footnote (six criteria listed unabbreviated) — pending]

GCF defined set of prohibited conduct in Fund-related Activities: Corruption, Fraud, Coercion, Collusion, Obstructive practice, Abuse, Retaliation against Whistleblowers/Witnesses, Money Laundering, Terrorist Financing. GCF maintains a zero-tolerance policy. NB existing acronym PP = prohibited practice (Programming Manual).

Any party that contributes to, executes, implements, bids for or participates in Fund-related Activities (contributor, AE, DAE, EE, delivery partner, fiscal agent, financial intermediary, vendor, etc.). (Same defined term in the AML/CFT policy.)

Conduct that violates GCF policies or involves significant risk to the GCF (harmful to its interests, reputation, operations or governance); includes Misconduct, Prohibited Practices and Conflicts of Interest. [review: ZH renders both 'Wrongdoing' and 'Misconduct' as 不当行为 (collision within the ZH translation).] Cross-ref: 'Misconduct' is now a distinct glossary entry (IIU — General Information Brochure); ZH 不当行为 collides between the two — kept distinct.
Observers with the meaning given in the Rules of Procedure of the Board; included within Covered Individuals (definition added by decision B.23/08).
Administrative sanction declaring a Counterparty ineligible (indefinitely or for a period) for future GCF financing, GCF-financed contracts, or other participation in GCF-related activity.
Sanction requiring a Counterparty to comply with remedial/preventive measures within set periods as a condition to avoid debarment; debarment may automatically take effect on non-compliance.
Any agreement entered into by an accredited entity and an Executing Entity for the purpose of implementing a Fund-related Activity.

Proactive measure by the Independent Integrity Unit to ensure Prohibited Practices are prevented in Fund-related Activities (distinct from reactive investigation of reports). [review: body-text phrase, not a defined term; FR/RU/AR are descriptive paraphrases (MED confidence); ES most stable.]

es.t: Grupo de Gestión de Riesgos de Integridad

items.note: GCF body (Executive Director, Head of IIU and senior Secretariat managers) providing strategy and direction to ensure GCF integrity policies are implemented and maintained. [review: RU official wording = «Группа управления репутационными рисками» (reputational, not 'integrity').]

Risk-based due-diligence measures the GCF applies to identify and verify Counterparties (incl. beneficial owners) before entering a relationship and assess ML/FT risk; must be completed and approved before any disbursement.
Any activity connected to the GCF that appears connected with money laundering, financing of terrorism, or other Prohibited Practice or criminal offence.
A report concerning a Suspicious Activity made to the Head of IIU for investigation and/or disclosure to Competent Authorities.
Annual assessment under IIU supervision identifying residual ML/FT risks to the GCF and assessing the effectiveness of the overall AML/CFT Program.

Disclosing to a Counterparty that a Suspicious Activity / suspicious transaction report or related information has been filed with GCF management or Competent Authorities in relation to a Fund-related Activity (prohibited under the GCF AML/CFT policy).

items.source: Procedures and guidelines of the IRM

items.source: Procedures and guidelines of the IRM
items.note: An assertion (per the IRM ToR) by a person, group or community that they have been or may be affected by adverse impacts due to a GCF funded project or programme.

items.source: Procedures and guidelines of the IRM
items.note: A person, group of persons or community filing (or on whose behalf is filed) a grievance or complaint with the IRM, or on whose behalf the IRM initiates proceedings.

items.source: Procedures and guidelines of the IRM
items.note: Participatory, voluntary IRM process to help parties find a mutually satisfactory solution to the concerns in a grievance/complaint; does not determine culpability and is not a compliance review.

items.source: Procedures and guidelines of the IRM
items.note: IRM process of compliance appraisal, investigation and reporting on whether a GCF funded project/programme violated or did not comply with GCF operational policies/procedures, causing adverse impacts to a complainant.

items.source: Procedures and guidelines of the IRM
items.note: Initial phase of a compliance review in which the IRM considers whether there is prima facie evidence of adverse impact through non-compliance with GCF operational policies/procedures.

items.source: Procedures and guidelines of the IRM
items.note: Second phase of a compliance review in which the IRM investigates in depth whether the complainant has been/may be adversely impacted through non-compliance.

items.source: Procedures and guidelines of the IRM
items.note: To set right or remedy an adverse impact caused by a GCF funded project/programme; where non-compliant, includes bringing the project/programme into compliance.

items.source: Procedures and guidelines of the IRM
items.note: Plan developed by the GCF Secretariat (following a Board decision on a final compliance report) setting steps for the GCF, AE or EE to bring a project/programme into compliance and/or provide redress.

items.source: Procedures and guidelines of the IRM

items.source: Procedures and guidelines of the IRM

items.source: Procedures and guidelines of the IRM

Impacts resulting from the incremental impact on areas/resources used or affected by the project, combined with other existing, planned or reasonably defined developments at the time risks are identified (GCF ESS usage).

Resources with which people identify as a reflection and expression of their constantly evolving values, beliefs, knowledge and traditions (GCF IP/ESS usage).

A two-way process beginning early in project planning, encouraging stakeholder feedback, ongoing, based on prior disclosure of accessible information in relevant local language, free of manipulation/coercion, documented and disclosed (GCF IP policy).
The principle GCF effectively applies and requires evidence of for activities affecting indigenous peoples; the Board finances only activities for which FPIC has been properly sought.

items.en_term: operating entity of the Financial Mechanism

fr.t: entité chargée du fonctionnement du mécanisme de financement / bras opérationnel du mécanisme de financement

es.t: entidad encargada del funcionamiento del mecanismo de financiación

items.note: GCF's formal legal status: designated as an operating entity of the financial mechanism under Article 11 of the UNFCCC, accountable to and functioning under the guidance of the COP.
The UNFCCC's mechanism for channelling climate finance to developing-country Parties under Article 11; GCF is designated an operating entity of it.

Structural component of the Fund: dedicated funding windows by theme. Initially the Fund has windows for adaptation and mitigation, with an integrated approach for cross-cutting projects; the Board may add, modify or remove windows.

fr.t: mécanisme particulier pour le secteur privé

items.note: A facility of the Fund that enables it to directly and indirectly finance private-sector mitigation and adaptation activities at national, regional and international levels, consistent with a country-driven approach. (Acronym PSF already in glossary; this is the founding-text key-term definition.) [review: GI (2011) predates the PSF brand — all langs are descriptive (RU 'Кредитная программа'=credit programme), not the branded 'PSF' proper noun; reconcile vs existing PSF entry.]
An important criterion for allocating Fund resources; the Fund may employ results-based financing including payment for verified results, in particular for incentivizing mitigation.
Allocation device whereby the Board uses minimum floors for adaptation resources directed to particularly vulnerable countries (LDCs, SIDS, African States), aiming for geographic balance.
Access modality whereby recipient countries nominate competent subnational, national and regional implementing entities for accreditation to receive funding directly, enhancing country ownership.

Access modality whereby recipient countries access the Fund through accredited international entities (UN agencies, MDBs, IFIs, regional institutions). COLLISION-NOTE: distinct from existing acronym IAE = international access entity / international accredited entity — this is the access-MODALITY term, not the entity.

An accountability body the Board establishes to work with the secretariat and report to the Board, investigating allegations of fraud and corruption. (Acronym IIU already in glossary; this is the founding-text key-term.)

An accountability mechanism reporting to the Board that receives complaints related to Fund operations and makes recommendations. (Acronym IRM = Independent Redress Mechanism already in glossary; this is the founding-text key-term.)
The country hosting the Fund, selected through an open and transparent process endorsed by the COP (Republic of Korea, Songdo, Incheon).

The World Bank serves as interim trustee for the Fund, subject to review three years after operationalization; manages the financial assets of the Fund per Board decisions. NB cf. 'Interim Trustee' (project-cycle sense) — same institution. [review: AR renders 'trustee' as أمين المجلس (lit. board registrar/secretary) — doc-specific; verify vs القيّم/الوصي in other docs.]

The Fund's first investment framework (decision B.07/06), comprising investment policies, investment strategy and portfolio targets, and investment guidelines (6 criteria, 24 coverage areas). COLLISION-NOTE: predecessor of the existing 'Investment framework' / 'Investment Framework for GCF-2' entries; kept as a distinct initial-period entry. See also 'investment framework for GCF-1'.
Component of the initial investment framework covering all grants, concessional loans and other instruments: minimum concessionality, grant-equivalent accounting, no crowding out of other finance.
Activity-based component of the initial investment framework, composed of the 6 investment criteria and 24 coverage areas used to assess programme/project proposals.
Investment principle: the Fund provides the minimum concessional funding (grant-equivalent subsidy element) necessary to make a project or programme viable; can be up to the full project cost.

items.source: Interim environmental and social safeguards (Performance standards of IFC)
items.note: The eight IFC Performance Standards (PS1 Assessment & management of E&S risks; PS2 Labour; PS3 Resource efficiency & pollution; PS4 Community health, safety & security; PS5 Land acquisition & involuntary resettlement; PS6 Biodiversity; PS7 Indigenous peoples; PS8 Cultural heritage) adopted by the Fund as its interim ESS.

items.source: Interim environmental and social safeguards (Performance standards of IFC)
items.note: The first IFC Performance Standard (Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks and Impacts), which applies to ALL funding proposals under the Fund's interim ESS; PS2-8 apply modularly as needed.

es.t: salvaguardias ambientales y sociales provisionales del GCF

items.source: Interim environmental and social safeguards (Performance standards of IFC)
items.note: The Fund's interim environmental and social safeguards: the IFC Performance Standards and Guidance Notes adopted on an interim basis until the Fund develops its own ESS, applied in a scaled risk-based, modular manner.

fr.t: approche basée sur les risques à grande échelle

es.t: enfoque basado en los riesgos y en función de la escala

zh.t: 基于风险的规模化方法

items.source: Initial guiding framework for the Fund's accreditation process
items.note: Method of applying the interim ESS commensurate to a project's level of risk (not one-size-fits-all), coupled with modular application so low-/no-risk projects are not overburdened.

Core fiduciary criteria assessing key administrative and financial capacities (management, financial management & accounting, internal/external audit, control framework, procurement, transparency & accountability) that all accredited entities must meet.

zh.t: 与项目管理相关的初始专业信托标准

items.note: A specialized fiduciary criterion covering capacity to identify/appraise projects, manage execution of funding proposals, and report on delivery (project preparation & appraisal; oversight & control; M&E; project-at-risk systems). NB cf. the re-accreditation-process abbreviation 'specialized fiduciary standard for project management (PM)'.

items.note: A specialized fiduciary criterion covering transparent grant award mechanisms: eligibility criteria & evaluation, award decision & procedures, public access to beneficiary information, transparent allocation of financial resources. NB cf. the re-accreditation '...grant award and/or funding allocation mechanisms (GA)' entry.

fr.t: système de détection de projets à risque

ru.t: система управления проектами сопряженными с рисками

items.note: A system/process required under the specialized fiduciary standards to flag early when a project develops problems that may interfere with achieving its objectives, and to respond to redress them.

items.source: Initial guiding framework for the Fund's accreditation process
items.note: A mandatory Stage I step in the accreditation process verifying an applicant entity's legal status, registration/permits, track record, institutional presence, and readiness against the Fund's fiduciary and ESS criteria.

items.source: Initial guiding framework for the Fund's accreditation process
items.note: Stage I accreditation track for subnational, national and regional applicant entities; involves no-objection and institutional assessment steps plus an optional readiness step.

items.source: Initial guiding framework for the Fund's accreditation process
items.note: Stage I accreditation track for international entities (UN agencies, MDBs, IFIs, regional institutions); requires only the institutional assessment and completeness check.

items.source: Initial guiding framework for the Fund's accreditation process
items.note: Independent professionals or specialised firms engaged by the Accreditation Panel to support in-depth review of individual accreditation applications, managed via a roster maintained by the Secretariat.

items.source: Performance measurement framework for REDD+ results based payments
items.note: A GCF funding modality under which the Fund disburses payments for verified REDD+ results, in accordance with UNFCCC decision 9/CP.19, requiring technically assessed results, a reference level, safeguards information and a national strategy.

items.source: Performance measurement framework for REDD+ results based payments
items.note: The GCF modality for paying for verified climate results (notably REDD+), delivered via the GCF REDD+ results-based payments pilot programme; paired with the general mitigation PMF. (Generic UNFCCC concept given a specific GCF programme form.)

fr.t: Niveau d’émission de référence / Niveau de référence

es.t: nivel de referencia de las emisiones / valor de referencia

zh.t: 参考排放水平 / 参考水平

items.source: Performance measurement framework for REDD+ results based payments
items.note: The benchmark, technically assessed per UNFCCC 13/CP.19, against which REDD+ emission reductions are measured for results-based payments.

fr.t: centre d’information REDD+ de la CCNUCC

es.t: centro de información de REDD+ de la CMNUCC

ar.t: مركز معلومات اتفاقية الأمم المتحدة الإطارية بشأن تغير المناخ الخاص بـ REDD+

items.source: Performance measurement framework for REDD+ results based payments
items.note: The UNFCCC information hub that serves as the source of information on REDD+ results and safeguards underpinning the Fund's REDD+ results-based payments.

Quantitative indicators and qualitative factors, mapped to activity-specific sub-criteria, that accredited entities address (where applicable) when demonstrating a proposal's performance against the six investment criteria.
Sub-criteria under each of the six investment criteria, applied on a case-by-case basis to assess a funding proposal's relevance and impact; accompanied by indicative assessment factors.

items.source: Initial general guidelines for country programmes
items.note: A country-led programming document developed under NDA/focal-point leadership engaging government, sub-national institutions, civil society and private sector; covers development/climate context, stakeholder roles, programming priorities and alignment with the Fund. (Acronym CP already in glossary; this captures the initial general guidelines key-term form.)

items.source: Updated Strategic Plan for the GCF 2020-2023
items.note: The GCF's initial resource mobilization period (its first funding period, preceding GCF-1). COLLISION: existing acronym IRM = Independent Redress Mechanism (Programming Manual) — a DISTINCT entry. Both kept; disambiguate by context.
GCF AML/CFT measure to identify and verify the full identity of a (potential) Counterparty. COLLISION: existing acronym KYC = know your customer (Programming Manual) — DISTINCT. Both kept.

items.source: Thematic brief: Investing in Great Green Wall countries
items.note: African Union-launched initiative (2007; abbreviated GGW) to restore 100 million hectares of degraded African landscape, combat desertification and help the Sahel mitigate/adapt to climate change; a recurring GCF investment target and a flagship initiative for GCF-GEF coordinated engagement.
